title: Get a work permit: If your spouse or common-law partner can’t apply online - Canada.ca
content:
## Get a work permit: If your spouse or common-law partner can’t apply online
Most people already in Canada have to apply online for a work permit. However, there are some people who may not be able to apply online.
To get a paper application, tell us why your spouse or common-law partner can’t apply online.
You need to enable Javascript to be able to use the tool.

## Apply on paper for a work permit (because of a disability)
Follow the steps below to apply on paper to get a work permit.

### Step 1: Get the application package
This package includes the application guide and all the forms they need to fill out.
Read the guide before you fill out the application form:
- Instruction Guide [IMM 5553]
Tips for downloading and completing the forms
- You can fill it out and save it using a computer.
- You’ll need Adobe Reader 10 or higher.
- You must also turn JavaScript on.
- Filling out the form on a computer is easier than filling it out by hand.
- Having trouble downloading the form? See, “ How do I download and open a PDF form? ”
- Document Checklist [IMM 5556] (opens in a new tab)
- Application to Change Conditions, Extend my Stay or Remain in Canada as a Worker [IMM 5710] (opens in a new tab)
- Statutory Declaration of Common-Law Union [IMM 5409] (opens in a new tab)
- Use of a Representative [IMM 5476] (opens in a new tab)
If they have questions about the forms, find answers to their questions in the Help Centre .

### Step 2: Prepare their application package
Make sure they submit all the pages of the form.
- Click the “Validate” button at the top or bottom of the form. This makes sure that they answered all the questions in the form and will help them avoid processing delays.
- This makes sure that they answered all the questions in the form and will help them avoid processing delays.
- A new page with barcodes and a signature line will automatically be added at the end of their form.
- Print, sign and date the form. Print on white, bond-quality, non-glossy paper. Use a laser printer.
- Print on white, bond-quality, non-glossy paper.
- Use a laser printer.
- Place the barcode page at the top of their package. If they have more than one package, place a separate barcode page on each one.
Find out more about the barcode form .
Depending on their situation, they may need to pay third parties for:
If they need either of these services, they’ll pay these fees directly to the third party.

### Step 3: Pay the application fees
In most cases, their fees will include processing fees for them and anyone they include on their application.
Our instruction guide can help them understand which fees apply and show them how to pay them.

### Step 4: Submit the application

#### Track your application
If you submit a paper application, we recommend that you use a postal or courier service with a tracking number to ensure you get proof that your application was delivered to the IRCC office.
Submit their completed application to the address given in the application package. Make sure they include the fee receipt and all the necessary documents.
If they can’t apply online or use the forms above because of a disability, they can ask for other formats including large print and braille. Contact the Client Support Centre (in Canada only) or submit a Web form for other formats.

## Apply on paper for a work permit (problem with the online application)
Follow the steps below to apply on paper to get a work permit.

### Step 1: Get the application package
This package includes the application guide and all the forms they need to fill out.
Read the guide before you fill out the application form:
- Instruction Guide [IMM 5553]
Tips for downloading and completing the forms
- You can fill it out and save it using a computer.

### Step 2: Prepare their application package
Make sure they submit all the pages of the form.
- Click the “Validate” button at the top or bottom of the form. This makes sure that they answered all the questions in the form and will help them avoid processing delays.
- This makes sure that they answered all the questions in the form and will help them avoid processing delays.

- Print, sign and date the form. Print on white, bond-quality, non-glossy paper. Use a laser printer.

- Place the barcode page at the top of their package. If they have more than one package, place a separate barcode page on each one.
Find out more about the barcode form .

If they need either of these services, they’ll pay these fees directly to the third party.

In most cases, their fees will include processing fees for them and anyone they include on their application.
Our instruction guide can help them understand which fees apply and show them how to pay them.

#### Track your application
If you submit a paper application, we recommend that you use a postal or courier service with a tracking number to ensure you get proof that your application was delivered to the IRCC office.
Submit their completed application to the address given in the application package. Make sure they include the fee receipt and all the necessary documents.
If they can’t apply online or use the forms above because of a disability, they can ask for other formats including large print and braille. Contact the Client Support Centre (in Canada only) or submit a Web form for other formats.
